In the _renderResult function, when the result is invalid, set `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` when valid, set `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an initial aria-expanded attribute set on page load. Screen reader users cannot know the initial state (collapsed) of the navigation.

In the mobileNav init function, set the initial aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button based on the initial state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have an initial aria-expanded attribute set on page load. Screen reader users cannot know the initial state (collapsed) of the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not trap focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer, causing navigation to be difficult.
issuesFound.fix:
Use a focus trap (like the createFocusTrap function) when opening the drawer, and release the trap when closing.
    Line ~2000: In the open function, after adding classes, create a focus trap with `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`. In the close function, deactivate the trap.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed (especially via the Escape key), focus does not return to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place on the page.
issuesFound.fix:
In the _closeInstance function, call trigger.focus() after closing the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The “hamburger” button (`.nav-toggle`) opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open()` function add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in the `close()` function add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion toggles panels via `faqAccordion.open()` / `close()`, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated. Users of screen readers cannot perceive which FAQ item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`[data-period]`) act as a two‑state toggle group, yet the script only toggles CSS classes and never updates `aria‑pressed`. This violates the ARIA button pattern for toggle controls.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid by calling `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)`, which only adds `aria-describedby`. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so assistive technologies are not informed of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()` (or after calling `srUtils.markInvalid`) add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when the field becomes valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, `_updateCountBadge(count)` updates the visible badge and its `aria-label`, but no ARIA live region is used, so screen‑reader users are not notified of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and, after updating the badge, set its text to “{count} products found”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period label changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, `_updatePlanCard()` updates the price text and period label, but no live region is used to announce the change. Users relying on screen readers may miss the price update.
issuesFound.fix: After updating `plan.priceDisplay.textContent`, also update a dedicated live region (e.g., `#price-live`) with a message like “{planName} price changed to {price} per {period}”.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates (e.g., search result count) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The search module updates the result count in `_updateStatus()` but only sets the text of a hidden status element without ensuring it has `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` at the time of update, which may prevent screen readers from announcing the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the status element (`_statusEl`) has `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` (or `assertive` as appropriate) before updating its `textContent`.  

These eight issues satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑E) and are directly observable from the source code with specific element selectors and line numbers.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The same `mobileNav.toggle()` function does not set `aria‑pressed` on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of its on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open));` whenever the drawer is opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger click handler missing aria‑expanded (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerClick` in the dropdown menu toggles panels but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger element, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After opening a dropdown, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and after closing, set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger keyboard handler missing aria‑expanded (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerKeydown` handles Enter/Space to open a dropdown but does not modify `aria‑expanded` on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Mirror the fix from Issue 3 inside the keydown branch that opens the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown is treated as a two‑state toggle but never receives `aria‑pressed` updates.
issuesFound.fix: When a dropdown is opened, set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and reset to `'false'` on close.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands an answer panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the associated trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria‑expanded (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.close(item)` collapses an answer panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the start of the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product‑filter count update not announced (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the product count badge (`_countBadge`) but does not announce the new count to a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, set its text in a hidden live region, e.g. `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products shown`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markInvalid()` adds an error description but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field, so AT does not convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `markInvalid`, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. Likewise, `markValid` should remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: During `dropdownMenu.init()`, each trigger button receives `aria-haspopup="true"` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the disclosure pattern on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In the registration loop, after setting `aria-haspopup`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded (JS‑E)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the trigger element receives `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑controls`, but the script never updates `aria‑expanded` when the dropdown is opened or closed. Assistive technologies cannot convey the current state of the menu.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ component toggles visibility of answer panels, but the trigger button (`[data-faq-trigger]`) never receives an updated `aria‑expanded` value. Users of assistive technology cannot tell which items are open.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)`, but `srUtils.markInvalid` only adds `aria‑describedby` and never sets `aria‑invalid="true"`. Consequently, screen readers are not informed that a field is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markValid` removes `aria‑describedby` but does not remove or set `aria‑invalid="false"`. Assistive technologies may continue to treat the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, `_updateCountBadge(count)` updates the visible badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement. Users relying on screen readers receive no feedback that the result count changed.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not announce changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates the UI (price numbers, labels, etc.) but does not inform assistive technology of the new billing period. Users cannot know whether they are viewing monthly, quarterly, or annual pricing.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes visually but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the state change. Screen readers will announce the button as collapsed even when the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);` in the open() function. Line 2185: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);` in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the attribute absent until the first toggle interaction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not trap focus or move focus when opened
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving into the drawer, and no focus trap is activated to keep keyboard users within the menu. The _trap variable is declared but never instantiated.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels open and close without updating the aria-expanded attribute on their trigger buttons, so screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));`. Line 2295: In _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not toggle aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes, preventing screen readers from announcing the popup state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2705: In _open(), add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 2715: In _close(), add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is created with role="combobox" but without the required aria-expanded="false" attribute for its initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800: In suggestionListRenderer.mount(), after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state but do not set aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot convey which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: In _syncToggleState(), inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch element (role="switch") lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, so screen readers cannot identify its purpose.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the error message is displayed but the input field is not marked with aria-invalid="true", so screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4050: In _renderResult(), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` inside the else block (when !result.valid), and add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` inside the if block (when result.valid).

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count and results update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content has changed.

Line 2980: In applyFilters(), after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    totalMatches === 0
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) indicate their active state only via CSS classes. Because they act as a single-select button group (similar to tabs), they must use aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid helper links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, and the error state is not exposed programmatically.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the markValid helper removes the error message reference but leaves aria-invalid="true" (or doesn't explicitly set it to false). This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1328: Add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price values update visually but screen reader users receive no confirmation of the change. A polite live region announcement is required to inform users that prices have been updated and what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2212: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });` add: `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close ignore announceToSR parameter

issuesFound.fix:
Line 2518: Add: `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(\`\${item.title} answer expanded\`);`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel uses role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", failing to communicate to assistive technology that the content underneath is inert while the dialog is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly places aria-modal on wrapper instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay wrapper div instead of the dialog element itself, which may cause assistive technology to incorrectly apply modal behavior to the backdrop rather than the dialog content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div and add it to the inner div with role="dialog": <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element in SkipLinks lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since the page contains multiple navigation landmarks (desktop nav, mobile nav, footer nav), screen reader users cannot distinguish the purpose of this specific navigation region without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: `<nav aria-label="Skip links">`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron character (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger is decorative and redundant since the button text already conveys the menu item purpose. Screen readers announce the arrow character as "black up-pointing triangle," creating unnecessary verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1128: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element wrapping the desktop navigation lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. With multiple navigation regions present on the page, users cannot distinguish this primary navigation from others via landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1102: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links indicating the current page (item.current) rely solely on visual styling (CSS class) to convey active state. Screen reader users cannot identify which page is current without the aria-current="page" attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element inside the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. This creates ambiguity when multiple navigation landmarks are present on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1175: `<nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in the mobile menu indicating the current page rely only on CSS classes for visual distinction. Without aria-current="page", screen reader users cannot identify the active location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop per its TypeScript interface, but ThemeToggle renders it without one. This creates an icon-only button with no accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users (WCAG 4.1.2, 2.4.4).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1242: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<section>` element containing the hero content lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since the page contains multiple section landmarks, this makes it difficult for screen reader users to identify the purpose of this specific region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1270: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing screen-reader text alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic value (e.g., "40,000+") is rendered as plain text without the valueLabel provided in the data interface (e.g., "40,000 plus"). Screen readers may misinterpret symbols like "+" and "%", causing confusion about the actual values.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1470: `

{stat.value}

`

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★/☆) inside StarRating are decorative since the parent span already provides an aria-label with the full rating text. However, these characters are not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "black star" repeatedly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1575: `{stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The Annual billing toggle button indicates selection state (value === 'annual') but lacks aria-pressed. Without this attribute, assistive technology cannot convey the selected state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1898: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the previous slide control lacks an aria-label prop, which is required by the IconButton interface. This results in an icon-only button with no accessible name, making it impossible for screen reader users to identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2078: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the next slide control lacks an aria-label prop. As an icon-only button, it provides no accessible name for screen reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 2.4.4.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2083: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator asterisk (*) is not marked with aria-hidden. While a visually hidden "(required)" text is provided, screen readers may still announce "star" or "asterisk" before the label, creating redundant or confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it displays a spinner and disables the button but does not communicate the loading state to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users may not understand why the button is unresponsive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 778: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`
